title: Die Evangelien-Geschichten der Homiliensammlung des Ms. Vernon
summary: The repository XML represents University of Michigan CME item CME00062, a complete encoding of Carl Horstmann's 1877 article in Archiv fuer das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 57, pp. 241-316. Horstmann extracts forty-five Middle English verse narratives and a short addendum from the Northern Homily Cycle and associated material in the Vernon Manuscript, while consulting John Small's 1862 edition and several other witnesses. The complete 500-canvas BSB journal object and complete 710-canvas Digital Bodleian Vernon object are cached. Every selected Vernon passage is mapped within that full manuscript bundle; the mapping is necessarily non-contiguous, and the addendum is separately located in the Cana homily. Public whole-object images were not found for the named comparison manuscripts on 2026-07-12, so request routes and bounded uncertainties are recorded instead of selected-page downloads.
review_notes: The Northern Homily Cycle, the Vernon codex, Horstmann's selected edition, Small's comparison edition, the U-M encoding, and digital facsimiles are modeled as distinct layers.

Complete local bundles retain every provider-published image, including material outside the corpus selection. work_portion locators associate the selected passages without redefining either bundle as a cropped facsimile.
The inherited NLS Adv. MS. 19.3.1 bundle was checked and excluded: it is the Heege Manuscript, not the Edinburgh Royal College of Physicians witness edited by Small.
Horstmann's closing dateline, Sagan i. J. 1876, is editorial place/date evidence; the volume title page establishes publication at Braunschweig in 1877.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00062
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00062; the file is 373,582 bytes.

The XML has two DIV1 divisions, forty-five DIV2 selections, eight nested DIV3 divisions, 7,393 L elements in 111 LG elements, three prose P elements, 98 notes, and seventy-seven PB elements. Printed p. 266 is represented by two PB milestones, so PB count is not physical page count.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00062
entities.description: A manually keyed, proofread TEI-like representation of Horstmann's complete article, including German editorial matter, Middle English verse, notes, and addendum.
notes: The encoding preserves Horstmann's duplicated printed number 15 for the Placidas item and continues with 17; it does not silently supply a number 16.

entities.label: Carl Horstmann's 1877 selection from the Vernon Homily Collection

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, 'Die Evangelien-Geschichten der Homiliensammlung des Ms. Vernon,' Archiv fuer das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 57 (31. Jahrgang, 1877): 241-316.
bibliographic.title: Die Evangelien-Geschichten der Homiliensammlung des Ms. Vernon
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv fuer das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen

notes: This is the immediate printed source represented by CME00062.

The article prints forty-five selected narratives in source order except that items 39 and 40 reverse their Vernon order; the addendum on p. 316 prints thirty-four lines from a separate location in the Cana homily.
Horstmann selects narratives from their homiletic settings and sometimes abridges within a selection; item 29c explicitly omits an introductory passage of about one hundred lines.

entities.label: BSB complete IIIF sequence for Archiv volume 57 (1877)
entities.description: A 500-canvas provider-published color sequence preserving the complete exposed journal object, not only Horstmann's article.
entities.reproduction_format: 500-canvas IIIF Presentation 2 object; cached as a ZIP containing all width-1600 JPEG derivatives, provider manifest, and exact source inventory
notes: Horstmann's article maps exactly to canvases 251-326, printed pp. 241-316; the volume title page is canvas 7.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS. Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript)
entities.description: A monumental late-fourteenth-century English parchment codex whose current fols. 167r-318r contain the Northern Homily Cycle and associated texts. Horstmann's selected passages occur discontinuously from current fol. 167r through fol. 215v, with the addendum at fol. 176r.

holding.notes: The origin and patronage remain debated; the current record tentatively gives England, West Midlands.

physical_description.extent: viii + 342 + i leaves, incomplete; originally viii + 413, with 71 leaves thought lost
physical_description.format: Large codex, normally quires of eight; nineteenth-century crushed russet leather over bevelled oak boards
notes: The current digital sequence jumps from fol. 126v to fol. 167r because forty-one leaves are missing at that point.
The complete codex contains more than 350 religious and didactic items, so the selected passages are only a small, discontinuous portion of the whole physical object.

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian Vernon Manuscript object
entities.description: The complete 710-canvas object exposed by Digital Bodleian, including covers, spine, flyleaves, blanks, every surviving digitized leaf, and adjacent texts. No dedicated head, tail, or fore-edge views are exposed.
entities.reproduction_format: 710 color IIIF canvases; complete provider object cached at an effective width of 1600 pixels

entities.label: John Small's English Metrical Homilies (1862)

bibliographic.citation: John Small, ed., English Metrical Homilies from Manuscripts of the Fourteenth Century (Edinburgh: William Paterson, 1862).
bibliographic.title: English Metrical Homilies from Manuscripts of the Fourteenth Century

notes: Small prints the fragmentary Royal College of Physicians manuscript through the Purification and supplies gaps from CUL MS Gg.5.31 and Bodleian MS. Ashmole 42.
Horstmann states that Vernon follows Small's Edinburgh witness through item 12, except that item 4 is absent there, and explicitly cites variant readings from Small.

entities.label: Internet Archive/Google scan of Small's 1862 English Metrical Homilies
entities.description: A complete 229-page PDF of the printed book with cached Internet Archive metadata and scandata sidecars.
entities.reproduction_format: 229-page PDF with item metadata and scan-structure sidecars

entities.label: Edinburgh, Royal College of Physicians, Cursor Mundi manuscript with Northern Homily Cycle fragment
alternate_labels: Royal College of Physicians MS of Cursor Mundi
entities.description: An early-fourteenth-century composite parchment fragment. The Northern Homily Cycle occupies fols. 16r-36v between Cursor Mundi fragments and ends early in the second Purification exemplum; three leaves are missing after fol. 24.

holding.shelfmark: Exact current call number not established
holding.notes: Small calls it a manuscript of Cursor Mundi belonging to the Royal College of Physicians; the College's public catalog page warns that not all holdings are listed.

physical_description.extent: 50 leaves total; Northern Homily Cycle at fols. 16r-36v, with three leaves missing after fol. 24
physical_description.format: Thin quarto; two columns; generally 43 or 45 lines
notes: Bequeathed to the College in 1741 by Dr John Drummond; generally treated as the earliest and least expanded surviving witness of the cycle.

entities.label: London, British Library, Harley MS 4196
entities.description: A late-fourteenth- or early-fifteenth-century parchment manuscript whose expanded Northern Homily Cycle occupies fols. 1r-132r. Horstmann compares this recension broadly and at items 29 and 45.

date_statements.label: repository date
date_statements.value: fourth quarter of the fourteenth century-first quarter of the fifteenth century

physical_description.extent: 258 leaves, plus flyleaves, pastedown, and stub; Northern Homily Cycle at fols. 1r-132r

entities.label: London, British Library, Cotton MS Tiberius E VII
entities.description: A fifteenth-century parchment manuscript preserving the expanded Northern Homily Cycle at fols. 101r-281r. Horstmann says its text agrees word for word with Harley 4196 for the comparison under discussion.

date_statements.label: repository date
date_statements.value: first half of the fifteenth century

physical_description.extent: 281 folios, now bound in two volumes; Northern Homily Cycle at fols. 101r-281r
notes: Damaged in the 1731 Cotton Library fire; thirty-five associated burnt fragments are now cataloged separately as Cotton MS Fragments XXXII/6.

entities.label: Cambridge, University Library, MS Dd.1.1
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century, mainly paper Northern Homily Cycle witness in a generally southern dialect. Horstmann lists it among other manuscripts but does not document a specific collation in this article.

holding.notes: Current public whole-object images and exact selected loci were not established.

notes: Published descriptions warn that pagination was revised after losses.

entities.label: Cambridge, University Library, MS Gg.5.31
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century northern parchment witness of the original Northern Homily Cycle. Small used it to supply passages missing from the Edinburgh fragment.

holding.notes: The expanded modern shelfmark resolves Horstmann's abbreviated Gg V reference; current public whole-object images were not found.

notes: The manuscript has significant losses and published descriptions distinguish old and current pagination.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS. Ashmole 42
entities.description: A mid-fourteenth-century English parchment manuscript devoted to the Northern Homily Cycle. Small used it to supplement gaps in the Edinburgh manuscript; Horstmann lists it among other witnesses.

physical_description.extent: The whole manuscript contains the Northern Homily Cycle

entities.label: London, British Library, Harley MS 2253
entities.description: A famous late-thirteenth- to early-fourteenth-century multilingual miscellany. Horstmann cites fol. 64 only for a different version of the Marina narrative associated with item 14; it is not presented here as a Northern Homily Cycle witness.

date_statements.label: repository date
date_statements.value: late thirteenth century-first half of the fourteenth century

physical_description.extent: 142 leaves plus flyleaves; comparison passage at edition-reported fol. 64

agents.name: Royal College of Physicians of Edinburgh

local_copies.label: Complete Bodleian Vernon Manuscript IIIF bundle

work_portion.label: All Vernon passages selected in CME00062, mapped within the complete manuscript
locators: Item 1: current fols. 167r-v, canvases 221-222 (Horstmann legacy fol. 126)
Placidas, printed as a second item 15: fols. 181r-183v, canvases 249-254
Item 29a-b: fols. 196r-197r, canvases 279-281; item 29c: fols. 197r-199r, canvases 281-285
Item 40: fols. 210v-211r, canvases 308-309 (manuscript order precedes item 39)
notes: These locators are intentionally non-contiguous and include the addendum separately; they do not imply that intervening leaves belong to the selected work.
Exact hardlink reuse of the existing complete CME00052 bundle; checksum and inode identity verified.
The bundle includes all 710 published canvases, covers, flyleaves, blanks, and spine, with adjacent texts retained. It has no dedicated edge views; effective profile is 1600 pixels wide.

Supporting comparison outside work_portion: the item 14 footnote's analogous Theodora narrative maps under the current Digital Bodleian structure to fols. 93r-v, canvases 171-172; it is not part of the forty-five-item selection.

access.access_method: Email the College library; quote Small's title, Cursor Mundi manuscript, Drummond bequest, and fols. 16r-36v
access.contact: library@rcpe.ac.uk; Royal College of Physicians of Edinburgh, 11 Queen Street, Edinburgh EH2 1JQ, United Kingdom
access.format: Request a full-object color facsimile of all 50 leaves plus covers, binding, spine, flyleaves, blanks, mounts, and edges if offered; identify the NHC portion as fols. 16r-36v
notes: No public whole-object digital surrogate or exact current call number was found. Do not request or cache only the selected NHC leaves when a whole physical-object reproduction is practical.

access.access_method: Use the British Library catalog record to request access or reproduction; request the complete 258-leaf object and all codex furniture, then map NHC fols. 1r-132r and item 45 at edition-reported fol. 54

access.format: No public whole-object facsimile found; full-object reproduction subject to repository feasibility and terms

editorial_practices.source_feature: Narrative exempla embedded in Gospel homilies and associated material
editorial_practices.representation: Forty-five separately numbered extracts plus an addendum
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann extracts narrative passages from their larger homiletic settings, occasionally omits internal matter, and supplies editorial headings. Item 29c omits an introductory passage of about one hundred lines; items 39 and 40 reverse manuscript order.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Parallel readings in Small 1862 and expanded Northern Homily Cycle witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: Footnotes, variant readings, and a recensional overview
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann compares the earliest section with Small and explicitly discusses Harley 4196, Cotton Tiberius E VII, and a few item-specific parallels; merely listed Dd.1.1, Gg.5.31, and Ashmole 42 are not promoted to undocumented full collations.

editorial_practices.representation: Italic type, preserved in CME as HI/@REND='italic'
editorial_practices.description: Many italics transparently supply expanded letters or contractions, but no explicit statement of editorial policy was located in the article or volume front matter. The function is therefore inferred rather than universally asserted.
notes: Compare individual italic expansions directly with the mapped manuscript canvas before using them as evidence about scribal appearance.

evidence.citation: Item-by-item comparison of Horstmann's 1877 transcription with official Vernon IIIF structures and page images.
evidence.locator: Forty-five item spans and addendum recorded under access:vernon
evidence.summary: Confirms every selected passage's current folio and canvas mapping, the non-contiguous structure, the addendum at fol. 176r, and the reversed manuscript order of article items 39-40.
notes: The legacy fol. 126/current fol. 167 crosswalk is inferred from exact-text identity plus the official forty-one-leaf lacuna and remains an explicit open question for authoritative confirmation.

evidence.summary: Provides current institutional terms referenced by the exact Vernon manifest alongside its CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution.

evidence.summary: Complete source for title-page data, introduction, edited text, parallel page ranges, and Google scan notice.

evidence.citation: John Small, English Metrical Homilies (1862), introduction and manuscript description.
evidence.summary: Describes the Edinburgh Royal College of Physicians manuscript, its 50 leaves, Cursor Mundi framing fragments, NHC fols. 16r-36v, losses, format, Drummond provenance, and supplied readings from Gg.5.31 and Ashmole 42.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata and scandata for englishmetrical00smalgoog.

evidence.citation: Anne B. Thompson, ed., The Northern Homily Cycle, Middle English Texts Series (2008), online introduction.
evidence.summary: Current scholarly synthesis for cycle date and northern origin, three-recension model, approximately twenty witnesses, the early Edinburgh text, Vernon/Simeon expansion, Harley/Cotton expansion, manuscript identities, and genre.
notes: The modern edition itself is copyrighted and linked for research; no local copy is cached or redistributed.

open_questions.question: What is the exact current call number of Small's Edinburgh Cursor Mundi/Northern Homily Cycle manuscript, and can RCPE supply a complete physical-object color facsimile under reusable terms?
next_steps: Email library@rcpe.ac.uk with Small's 1862 description and request all 50 leaves plus covers, binding, spine, flyleaves, blanks, mounts, and edges if offered; ask separately for fols. 16r-36v mapping, price, resolution, and publication/commercial-use terms.

open_questions.question: Can a Bodleian or scholarly concordance explicitly confirm that Horstmann's first legacy fol. 126 corresponds to current Vernon fol. 167r after the forty-one-leaf lacuna?
next_steps: Consult the full Vernon catalog bibliography or ask Bodleian manuscripts staff for the foliation history; retain both locators and do not replace current fol. 167r with legacy fol. 126.

open_questions.question: What exact current folios contain the corresponding selected passages in Dd.1.1, Gg.5.31, Ashmole 42, and the item 29 comparison in Harley 4196?
next_steps: Request current foliations and contents concordances from CUL, Bodleian, and BL before adding item-level mappings; never infer loci from Horstmann's broad witness list.

open_questions.question: Did Horstmann state anywhere outside this article that every italic letter in the transcription represents an expanded manuscript abbreviation?
next_steps: Search Horstmann's methodological prefaces and compare each queried italic span with the mapped Vernon canvas; record exceptions if found.

open_questions.question: Is the second printed number 15 for Placidas a typographical error for 16, or an intentional numbering artifact?
next_steps: Preserve the printed duplicate 15 in source-facing data and use sequence position 16 only as an explicit normalized convenience unless contemporary errata or later scholarship resolves it.
